--- SOURCE 1: GeneConvene Global Collaborative | FNIH ---
URL: https://fnih.org/our-programs/geneconvene-global-collaborative/

SUMMARY:
GeneConvene offers technical information, advice, training, and coordination for research on gene drive and other genetic biocontrol technologies.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: Home • GeneConvene Virtual Institute ---
URL: https://www.geneconvenevi.org/home/

SUMMARY:
The Virtual Institute is an initiative of the GeneConvene Global Collaborative, a program within the Foundation for the National Institutes of Health.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SUMMARY:

--- SOURCE 4: The organizational structure of global gene drive research ---
URL: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0959378024000062

SUMMARY:
This text is the first to develop a systematic account of the organizational structure that underpins global gene drive research.

URL: https://fnih.org/our-programs/geneconvene-global-collaborative/gene-drive-research-forum/

SUMMARY:
The Gene Drive Research Forum provides a unique environment for interaction among a broad spectrum of stakeholders for engineered gene drive technologies.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Global initiatives like GeneConvene foster cross-border partnerships, enhancing research, sharing resources, and promoting standardized practices in gene drive technology development.
Different countries have unique regulatory frameworks impacting gene drive applications, necessitating tailored approaches for compliance and ethical considerations in various jurisdictions.
Engaging communities is crucial for gene drive technologies; public understanding and acceptance influence policy decisions and the success of field applications.
Ongoing studies aim to refine gene drive mechanisms, assess ecological impacts, and explore innovative applications, ensuring responsible advancement of this transformative technology.
